settings.heading: Help Centre
settings.text: Find practical support guidance for orders, purchases, Business Health Check, Core Services, Business NBN, membership, marketplace pathways and contacting RBP.

settings.heading: Common support areas
settings.text: Use these topics to decide what information to include when contacting support.

settings.title: Account access
settings.text: Include the email address used with RBP, the access issue and any order or membership context.

settings.title: Orders and purchases
settings.text: Include the order number, product or service name, delivery issue and preferred contact method.

settings.title: Business Health Check
settings.text: Include the business context, the issue you want reviewed and any deadlines or constraints.

settings.title: Core Services
settings.text: Include the service being considered, current business problem and whether you need advice, intake or delivery support.

settings.title: Business NBN support
settings.text: Include the business address, current service status, provider if known, activation timing, preferred contact method and urgent operational constraints.

settings.title: Membership
settings.text: Ask RBP to confirm current membership availability, inclusions, application steps and support pathways.

settings.title: Marketplace and partner offers
settings.text: Partner offers are subject to partner terms, eligibility and availability. RBP does not guarantee offer access or approval.

settings.title: Contact and support
settings.text: Use contact support when the correct product, service, marketplace pathway or next step is unclear.

settings.heading: Support questions
settings.text: Starter support articles are provided here while Help Centre metaobject articles are populated in Shopify Admin.

settings.question: How to access your account
settings.answer: Customers can access their account using the account link in the store header. If you cannot access your account, contact RBP with the email address used for your order or enquiry. Do not send passwords or verification codes through the contact form.

settings.question: What happens after checkout
settings.answer: After checkout, your order confirmation will be sent to the email used at checkout. Some products are self-serve, while others require intake, manual review or support before delivery. Check the product page for fulfilment notes.

settings.question: What to include before requesting a Business Health Check
settings.answer: Include your business name, the issue or decision you are reviewing, timing pressure, relevant documents if requested, and the outcome you are trying to clarify. Do not provide sensitive passwords, financial account access, or confidential legal material unless specifically requested through a secure process.

settings.question: Choosing the right Core Service
settings.answer: Use Business Health Check for broad review, Business Advisor for owner decision support, Decision Desk for structured decision review, and The Fixer for practical triage of a specific issue. RBP does not provide legal, tax, accounting, financial or insurance advice unless separately arranged with an appropriate provider.

settings.question: What to provide for Business NBN support
settings.answer: Include the business address, current provider if known, current connection status, activation timing, preferred contact method and any operational urgency. Address acceptance on the website does not confirm final serviceability, speed, network technology or activation.

settings.question: How membership enquiries work
settings.answer: Membership enquiries currently route through RBP support. Use the contact form to explain your business, what support you are looking for and any specific resources or services you want access to. RBP will respond with the appropriate next step.

settings.question: How partner pathways work
settings.answer: Marketplace and partner pathways may depend on eligibility, provider terms, availability and approval. RBP may help route enquiries but does not guarantee partner acceptance, offer availability, funding, insurance, legal or commercial outcomes.

settings.question: What to include in a support request
settings.answer: Include your name, email, business name, product or service involved, order number if available, preferred contact method, timing/deadline and a short summary of the issue. Do not send passwords, card numbers, tax file numbers or other highly sensitive information.
